title: TON Price Jumps as Telegram Plans to Rebrand Token to Gram
summary: Telegram is taking over The Open Network (TON) and plans to rename its token from Toncoin to Gram, causing a significant price increase. The rebranding is a return to the original name planned for the project before Telegram's previous involvement ended.
body:
The price of Toncoin surged after Telegram CEO Pavel Durov announced that the company will rebrand The Open Network's token, currently known as Toncoin, back to its originally intended name, Gram. The announcement came as part of a broader plan for Telegram to take the reins of the TON blockchain, years after the company abandoned the project following regulatory pressure from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission over its 2018 Gram token sale.

Telegram originally conceived TON as a scalable blockchain platform integrated with its messaging app, but withdrew after settling with the SEC in 2020. The project continued under the stewardship of the TON Foundation and independent developers. Now, Durov said Telegram will take over the TON Association and reposition the token—apparently aiming to reassert control and push for deeper integration of the Gram token within Telegram's ecosystem, which has over 900 million monthly active users.

For everyday users, this rebranding could signal renewed efforts to bring cryptocurrency payments and decentralized services directly into the Telegram app. If successful, the Gram token could see widespread adoption for transactions, tipping, and third-party app purchases. The move may also attract more developers to build decentralized applications on the TON blockchain, though it remains to be seen how the community and regulators will respond.

Investors and users should watch for official details from Telegram regarding the rebranding timeline, token migration plans, and any new features or integrations tied to Gram. The price of Toncoin is likely to remain volatile as the market reacts to these developments. Those interested in the project should track announcements from Telegram and the TON Foundation.
twitterThread: Telegram CEO Pavel Durov announces TON token will be rebranded back to 'Gram'—the original name before the SEC forced Telegram to abandon the project in 2020. Price is already pumping.
Now Telegram is taking control of the TON Association directly, years after walking away. The plan is to integrate Gram deeply into its 900M+ user app for payments, tips, and dApps.

Big questions remain: how will the existing TON community react, and what about regulators? If you hold Toncoin, watch for official migration and timeline details. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-01-ton-price-jumps-as-telegram-plans-to-rebrand-token-to-gram
standaloneTweet: Telegram CEO Durov says TON token will be renamed to Gram—the same name that got the project sued by the SEC in 2020. Price surges as Telegram retakes control of the blockchain. What's next for Gram?
